title: Start a container that's a duplicate of the host?
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

Lennart posted an interesting blog post recently:

https://0pointer.net/blog/running-an-container-off-the-host-usr.html

This creates a container that is an exact copy of the host system, useful as Lennart says, sometimes you just want a duplicate throw-away copy of the host system to try something without impacting the host rootfs. And constructing a new container with package managers takes time. Whether it is volatile or not probably isn't the most important but... Can you do something similar with podman? Start a container that's identical to the host asap? Just wondering... Because that would be very useful...

comments.body:
Yes that should be possible with --rootfs option and overlayfs.
`podman run --rootfs /:O echo hello` but I think this requires root.

I am sure there other other also ways to do with podman.

Got this error when I tried, I'm on pretty much latest Fedora 35 versions of everything:

```
$ sudo podman run --rootfs /:O /bin/bash
Error: error running container create option: stat /:O: no such file or directory
```

That might be 4.0 feature. You could try without :O but then your changes would also affect the host system.
Overlay on rootfs is definitley 4.0.  We should add this to the cool series blog.
It's definitely cool to me! I'm happy to close this if you guys want, I'll remember to try again when I upgrade to 4.0
